v2_sources.title: Sintilimab plus chemotherapy for unresectable gastric or gastroesophageal junction cancer: the ORIENT-16 Randomized Clinical Trial - Supplemental Online Content
v2_sources.citation: Sintilimab plus chemotherapy for unresectable gastric or gastroesophageal junction cancer: the ORIENT-16 Randomized Clinical Trial - Supplemental Online Content. JAMA (Journal of the American Medical Association). 2023.

boundary_tags.population_or_model: Patients with unresectable gastric or gastroesophageal junction cancer

v2_facts.definition: {'content': 'In PD-L1 CPS ≥5 patients, median OS was 18.7 months (95% CI, 14.8-NC) in sintilimab group vs 12.9 months (95% CI, 11.1-15.4) in placebo group; stratified HR 0.658 (95% CI, 0.502-0.861), p

v2_facts.definition: {'content': 'In all randomized patients, median OS was 15.4 months (95% CI, 13.0-18.4) in sintilimab group vs 12.3 months (95% CI, 11.3-13.8) in placebo group; stratified HR 0.761 (95% CI, 0.622-0.931

v2_facts.definition: {'content': 'In PD-L1 CPS ≥5 patients, confirmed objective response rate was 63.6% (95% CI, 56.2-71.0) in sintilimab group vs 49.4% (95% CI, 41.8-57.0) in placebo group; difference 13.91% (95% CI, 3.5

v2_facts.definition: {'content': 'In all randomized patients, confirmed objective response rate was 58.2% (95% CI, 52.3-64.2) in sintilimab group vs 48.4% (95% CI, 42.3-54.6) in placebo group; difference 9.60% (95% CI, 1.
v2_facts.definition_zh: {'content': 'In all randomized patients, confirmed objective response rate was 58.2% (95% CI, 52.3-64.2) in sintilimab group vs 48.4% (95% CI, 42.3-54.6) in placebo group; difference 9.60% (95% CI, 1.

v2_facts.definition: {'content': 'Duration of response in CPS ≥5 patients: median 9.8 months (95% CI, 7.1-21.1) vs 7.1 months (95% CI, 5.4-9.0); stratified HR 0.62 (95% CI, 0.42-0.91), p=0.01', 'page': 7}
v2_facts.definition_zh: {'content': 'Duration of response in CPS ≥5 patients: median 9.8 months (95% CI, 7.1-21.1) vs 7.1 months (95% CI, 5.4-9.0); stratified HR 0.62 (95% CI, 0.42-0.91), p=0.01', 'page': 7}

v2_facts.definition: {'content': 'Any treatment-related adverse events leading to treatment discontinuation: 9.8% (32/328) in sintilimab group vs 5.6% (18/320) in placebo group', 'page': 11}
v2_facts.definition_zh: {'content': 'Any treatment-related adverse events leading to treatment discontinuation: 9.8% (32/328) in sintilimab group vs 5.6% (18/320) in placebo group', 'page': 11}

v2_facts.definition: {'content': 'Any treatment-related serious adverse events: 26.2% (86/328) in sintilimab group vs 21.9% (70/320) in placebo group', 'page': 12}
v2_facts.definition_zh: {'content': 'Any treatment-related serious adverse events: 26.2% (86/328) in sintilimab group vs 21.9% (70/320) in placebo group', 'page': 12}

v2_facts.definition: {'content': 'Treatment-related adverse events leading to death: 1.8% (6/328) in sintilimab group vs 0.6% (2/320) in placebo group', 'page': 13}
v2_facts.definition_zh: {'content': 'Treatment-related adverse events leading to death: 1.8% (6/328) in sintilimab group vs 0.6% (2/320) in placebo group', 'page': 13}

v2_facts.definition: {'content': 'Subsequent anticancer therapy rates were lower in sintilimab group (36.4%) vs placebo group (47.4%), suggesting delayed disease progression', 'page': 6}
v2_facts.definition_zh: {'content': 'Subsequent anticancer therapy rates were lower in sintilimab group (36.4%) vs placebo group (47.4%), suggesting delayed disease progression', 'page': 6}
